decision: Add ONE reusable workflow `.github/workflows/cut-audit-seed.yml` to SuxOS/.github, triggered `on: workflow_call` (inputs: `repo`, `tag`), plus a documented 6-line caller stub (`on: release: types:[published]`) in the workflow header for release-cutting repos (sux first) to adopt separately. Behavior, all via `gh api` (GITHUB_TOKEN), no new engine: (1) derive arc id `v{major}.{minor}` from the release tag (e.g. `v1.1.0` → `v1.1`, matching the retired `drummer-v1-1-*` naming); (2) `gh api repos/{repo}/milestones?state=all` → if no milestone titled `v{major}.{minor}-cut-audit`, create it, else reuse (milestone is the ONLY done-record, per registry-is-truth); (3) search that repo for an open OR closed issue titled `Cut-audit: v{major}.{minor}` — if absent, create one assigned to the milestone, body referencing the arc doc (`docs/design/2026-07-suxos-v{N}-arc.md`) and instructing the drain to report GREEN/AMBER to the owner; if present, no-op. decision: Add a per-workflow consecutive-red-streak collector to `.github/workflows/fabric-health.yml`, computed at WORKFLOW-CONCLUSION granularity (not job granularity — the standalone `.github/actions/red-streak` composite from #728 is deliberately NOT reused here; it needs per-workflow `job-name`/`job-timeout-minutes` config that doesn't exist org-wide and is the right tool only for the job-level issue-build spend breaker). Concrete spec: (1) In the existing per-repo loop, extend the already-fetched `batch_runs=gh run list --repo $slug --limit 100 --json workflowDatabaseId,conclusion,status` (gh returns newest-first). For each workflow id, walk its completed runs in the window newest→oldest and count CONSECUTIVE reds, adopting the red-streak action's classification semantics: `failure`/`timed_out` = red (extend), `cancelled` = NEUTRAL (skip — this is the load-bearing timeout-vs-user-cancel ambiguity from #718/#725, and workflow-level granularity can't disambiguate, so treat as neither red nor reset), `success`/any other terminal conclusion = reset (stop). (2) Window-truncation guard (#345): only when a workflow's entire visible history in the 100-run window is red AND that visible count is below the alert threshold, issue ONE targeted `gh run list --workflow <id> --limit <threshold+1>` fallback to resolve the streak precisely — the identical batch-then-fallback shape the file already uses for `workflow_red`, so no new #350 API-cost regression. (3) Emit `suxos_workflow_red_streak{repo,workflow} value=<n>` per workflow and `suxos_workflow_red_streak_max{repo}` per repo, through the SAME InfluxDB line-protocol path and gated on the SAME `runs_ok`/`collection_ok` flag as `workflow_red` (satisfies the #305 collection-integrity contract — absent sample on a degraded query, never a fabricated 0). (4) Add an NTFY predicate: any `suxos_workflow_red_streak >= RED_STREAK_THRESHOLD` fires, with `RED_STREAK_THRESHOLD=3` (matches the red-streak action default; the 10-run gardener streak would have tripped at run 3). (5) Add the two new series names to `scripts/test-dashboard-queries.sh` and add a Grafana panel series. Streak values are reported as lower bounds by construction, which is sufficient because the alert only cares that count >= threshold.
rationale: The gardener root cause is fixed (vault#13) and the actuator shipped (#728/#725); the only surviving ask is the detector, and it is a pure design/scope decision — no secret, console, payment, or physical act blocks it. Colin's own thread already narrowed it to a buildable statement and isolated the single hard tradeoff (ordered per-workflow streak vs #345 under-reporting vs #350 API cost). That tradeoff has a clean answer: reuse the file's existing proven batch-then-fallback machinery, its collection_ok gating, and its NTFY/InfluxDB paths, adding only the streak walk and one bounded fallback. Choosing workflow-conclusion granularity over reusing the job-level red-streak action is the least-surprising senior call: the action requires per-workflow timeout config that doesn't exist and is aimed at a different (job-timeout) question, whereas 'a collector went dark for N runs' is inherently a workflow-conclusion question. Scope is a single-file change plus a test + dashboard update — small, independently buildable, gate-passing.

decision: Build a single new workflow `arc-milestone-seed.yml` in SuxOS/.github plus a small deterministic helper script — NOT a markdown-table/workstream parser. Scope, pinned: (1) Trigger: `on: push` to `main` with `paths: [docs/design/**-arc.md]` (also `workflow_dispatch` with a `doc_path` input for backfill). (2) For each added/modified arc doc in the push, ensure a GitHub milestone in THIS repo (.github) named from the doc's H1 title (fallback: the filename), idempotently — search existing milestones by title first, create only if absent; never duplicate. (3) Upsert exactly ONE umbrella "decompose this arc" issue in .github, using the marker-based single-issue upsert pattern already proven in fabric-health.yml: idempotency key is an HTML-comment marker `<!-- arc-seed: <doc_path> -->` grepped across open+closed issues; create only if none exists. The umbrella body is self-contained (builder token can't read cross-repo): it embeds the doc path, quotes the arc's build-order line (the `**W0 … → W10.**` line under §3) and the §7 release-gating "reopen folded issues rather than duplicating" instruction, links the milestone, and instructs a builder to decompose §3 into pipeline issues in the OWNING repos (per the doc's own Owning-repo column), honoring per-issue "verify against HEAD before re-implementing" warnings. Assign the umbrella to the milestone; label `enhancement` so the issue-build loop auto-picks it. (4) The umbrella IS the seed; the existing loop does the reasoning-shaped decomposition on pickup. When its child pipeline issues drain and it closes, the milestone closes = the done-record. Add a one-paragraph note to docs/design/2026-07-23-standing-automation.md §"Per-arc release cadence" recording that the Implement leg is this deterministic seeder + loop-driven decomposition. Rationale for the split: robustly parsing an arc table into correctly-linked, dependency-ordered, cross-repo, reopen-not-duplicate issues is a model-reasoning task (which is exactly why the prior deterministic build timed out); the deterministic layer should only do what is genuinely deterministic (milestone ensure + single-issue upsert), and hand the judgment to the loop it already has.
rationale: Doctrine-mandated Implement leg that is not yet built and not superseded. The needs-human timeout was caused by over-scoping toward a full arc-table parser + cross-repo issue synthesis (a reasoning task). Re-scoping to a deterministic milestone-ensure + marker-idempotent umbrella-issue upsert — a resolution the issue body itself sanctions ("if parsing ambiguous, seed only an umbrella issue … never guess workstreams") — makes it single-workflow, gate-passing, and reuses fabric-health.yml's existing upsert pattern. No human decision is the blocker; only a scoping choice was, and I made it. Relabel + body-rewrite prevents the builder from re-attempting the full parser and re-timing-out.

decision:
Implement an intentional-divergence ack for check-settings-drift.py using an item-set model (a superset of the issue's single-fingerprint idea, needed to satisfy "acked+extra → 🔴 on the extra"):

1. ACK FILE: `~/.claude/.drift-ack` (JSON). Schema:
   {"acked": ["deny:-<rule>", "deny:+<rule>", "hook:-<event>:<cmd>", "scalar:defaultMode", ...],
    "fingerprint": "<sha256 of sorted acked list>", "since": "2026-07-22", "expires": "2026-08-22"|null, "note": "rails off by choice"}
   `acked` is the canonical set of drift-item KEYS the operator is accepting. Store both the explicit list AND a sha256 of it (the "fingerprint" the issue asks for) so a hand-edited/stale ack is detectable.

2. CANONICAL DRIFT-ITEM KEYS: add a helper `_drift_items(live, repo) -> set[str]` that emits one stable string per drifted atom: `deny:-<rule>` (repo rule missing from live), `deny:+<rule>` (live-only rule), `hook:-<event>:<cmd>`, `hook:+<event>:<cmd>`, `scalar:defaultMode`, `scalar:disableClaudeAiConnectors`. The existing critical/config computation is refactored to derive from this item set so classification and ack use ONE source of truth.

3. CLASSIFICATION with ack: compute `current = _drift_items()`. If empty → silent (exit 0, byte-identical to today). If non-empty and a valid non-expired ack is present, split: `acked_now = current ∩ ack.acked` → render 🟡 "acknowledged divergence (rails off by choice since <since>)"; `extra = current − ack.acked` → still 🔴 SECURITY-CRITICAL. If no valid ack → today's behavior exactly. So acked-drift→🟡, acked+extra→🔴 on the extra only.

4. ACK VALIDITY (fail toward 🔴 — the safe direction, opposite of the hook's overall fail-open): ack ignored (full 🔴) when file absent, unparseable JSON, `fingerprint` != sha256(sorted acked), or `expires` present and past (parse ISO date; any parse error → treat as expired/ignored). Add a one-line note when an ack was present-but-invalid so it's visible, not silent.

5. install.sh --apply GUARD: before merging deny/hooks into live, if a VALID non-expired `~/.claude/.drift-ack` exists, refuse and print "a drift ack is active (rails off by choice since <since>); clear ~/.claude/.drift-ack or re-run with --force to override" and exit non-zero. New `--force` flag bypasses. Keep it minimal — one guard block near the top of the apply path (install.sh:115 area).

6. KEEP the hook fail-OPEN overall (bare `except: sys.exit(0)` stays); only ack-application fails toward showing 🔴.

7. TESTS (per tests/fixtures + tests/test_hooks.sh convention, ~line 1554 CSD block): (a) acked-drift → 🟡, no 🔴 section; (b) acked + one extra deny-removal → 🔴 lists ONLY the extra, 🟡 lists the acked; (c) no ack → output byte-identical to current behavior; (d) expired ack → full 🔴; (e) fingerprint-mismatch ack → full 🔴; (f) malformed ack JSON → full 🔴 + never exit 2. Drive via stdin SessionStart envelope with a temp HOME so the hook reads a fixture live/repo pair; assert exit 0 always.

8. DOCS: add a `.drift-ack` subsection to home/.claude/settings.README.md — schema, how to mint one (write the current `_drift_items()` output + fingerprint), expiry semantics, and that --apply refuses while active.
rationale: The issue is fully specified (problem, proposal, acceptance) and needs only ordinary engineering plus a handful of design defaults, all of which I've fixed above (ack path/schema, item-set-vs-single-fingerprint model, fail-toward-🔴 on invalid ack, --apply guard with --force, expiry semantics). No secret, console bootstrap, payment, or physical act is involved, so it is not HARD_BLOCKED. The `needs-human` label originates entirely from the build loop repeatedly hitting the 30-minute job timeout (the bot's own final comment attributes it to SuxOS/.github#701's timeout backstop and explicitly says a normal build/scoping doc is legitimate) — a churn artifact, not a real block. Right-sized as one issue: ~one hook refactor + one install.sh guard + fixture tests + README note.

decision: Add a content-preservation (patch-id) check to home/.claude/hooks/audit-git-consequences.py and gate BOTH discard-alarm paths on it. Concretely: (1) New helper `_content_preserved(sha, cwd) -> Optional[bool]`. Compute the target commit's own patch-id via `git diff-tree --stdin`/`git show <sha> | git patch-id --stable` (first field of output). Then in ONE pass build the set of patch-ids present in a bounded recent window across all refs: `git log --all --no-merges -p -n 200 | git patch-id --stable`, collect the patch-id column into a set, and return True iff the target's patch-id is in that set. Return None on ANY failure (git subprocess nonzero/None, empty patch-id for an empty/binary-only diff, timeout) — conservative-by-construction, never suppress on error. Use the existing `git_out`/`git_returncode` helpers from _hookutil.py; add a subprocess timeout consistent with the other hook git calls. (2) In `_consequences`, gate the branch-removed alarm (lines ~140-144): only append the 'discarded' message when `_reachable(old_sha) is False AND _content_preserved(old_sha) is not True` (i.e. content NOT confirmed preserved). (3) Replace the blunt `prev_gone` short-circuit (lines 138-139): instead of unconditional `continue` when `ref in prev_gone`, run the same reachable+content check — suppress only when content is preserved OR still reachable; otherwise fall through to the alarm (fixes the false-NEGATIVE counterpart the owner confirmed in the comment: `git branch -D` of never-merged work whose remote branch was deleted). (4) Apply the identical content gate to the non-fast-forward move alarm (lines ~151-156) so rebase-with-conflict-resolution that preserves patch-id is downgraded too. (5) Tests: extend tests/test_hooks.sh with a REAL git fixture per the #117 real-shape doctrine — a genuine cherry-pick+branch -D (patch-id preserved → no alarm) AND a genuine orphan-commit discard (patch-id absent → alarm still fires), building the unreachable-object fixture with `git reflog expire --expire=now --all && git gc -q --prune=now` per the documented pattern. Window size 200 is the default from the issue; keep it a module constant. This is the whole scope — no separate scoping doc needed.
rationale: The issue names an exact file, exact lines, exact mechanism (git patch-id), and an explicit conservative-fail rule; the owner's comment closes the one open design question (also tighten the prev_gone false-negative in the same pass). All the substrate already exists on HEAD — `_reachable`, `git_out`, `git_returncode` in _hookutil.py, the tests/test_hooks.sh + fixtures harness, and the documented reflog/gc trick for real unreachable-object fixtures. The only thing to decide was HOW to compute/scope the patch-id lookup (single-pass `git log --all -p -n 200 | git patch-id --stable` membership) and that error/empty-diff maps to 'keep the loud flag' — both decided here. The prior 35+ build attempts failed on the 30-min gate timeout (a builder spinning, most likely on getting a real cherry-pick fixture green), which is a builder-execution problem the concrete fixture recipe above resolves, not a reason for human involvement. No secret, payment, console bootstrap, or physical act is required.

decision: Root cause is now definitively isolated (not the theories in the issue body): the GRAFANA_PROM_URL GitHub Actions secret on SuxOS/.github is a MALFORMED URL string, not a rotated/expired token. Live proof from the latest self-fabric-health run (30103896378, 2026-07-24T15:08Z): "grafana prometheus push HTTP 000 (curl exit 3)" — curl exit 3 = URL-parse failure (stray whitespace/newline/illegal char in the stored value), NOT DNS(6)/connect(7)/TLS(35)/timeout(28). The very same step's Loki push returns HTTP 204, and Loki auth uses the SHARED GRAFANA_LOKI_TOKEN — so the token is proven valid and theory #2 (token rotation) is ruled out; only GRAFANA_PROM_URL is broken. Fix = two atoms: (1) Re-set the GRAFANA_PROM_URL repo secret to the clean canonical value `https://prometheus-prod-36-prod-us-west-0.grafana.net/api/v1/push/influx/write` (the exact endpoint sux/sux/src/grafana.ts pushes to via its own GRAFANA_PROM_URL) — a public endpoint URL, not a credential to mint; strip any trailing newline/space. (2) Harden fabric-health.yml push step (~line 1041-1056): change the non-2xx branch from a soft `::warning::` to failing the job (`exit 1`) BUT ONLY inside the existing `[ -n GRAFANA_PROM_URL ] && ...` gate, so the documented dormant-secrets path stays a green no-op; and add the round-trip read-back verify already specced as the #694 "effect" invariant in docs/design/2026-07-23-pipeline-invariant-registry-design.md (invariants/effect/grafana_push_verify.py — assert suxos_collector_ok has a fresh sample within one push cadence). Do the secret re-set FIRST so the newly-hard-failing job doesn't flip self-fabric-health red on every 15-min cron.

recommended_action: Remove needs-human, relabel, and dispatch as two slices. Slice A (dispatch-ready NOW, zero external provisioning): backup-on-write R2 mirror + durability-invariant test. Slice B: GRANTS_KV namespace separation, with the namespace minted via the Cloudflare MCP kv_namespace_create tool (NOT a human atom) and its ID committed to wrangler.jsonc.
decision:
Build BOTH options 1+2 from the issue (the issue's own recommendation), split into two independently-landable slices:

SLICE A — R2 backup-on-write (dispatch-ready immediately, env.R2 already bound at mychart.ts:312): Add `backupGrant(env, org, grant)` that, on every grant write, mirrors the grant JSON to private R2 under a versioned key `phi/mychart/<org>/_grant/<issued_at>.json` (reuse the existing PHI_PREFIX private-R2 path — grants are refresh tokens, so private bucket, never the public one). Call it at the two grant-write sites: mychart.ts:236 (refresh rotation) and :1496 (initial connect). Fire only on an actual write/rotation, never on a no-op refresh. Guard on `if (!env.R2) return` so it degrades to a no-op when R2 is unbound (matches existing PHI-write convention). Add tests: (1) durability invariant — the grant key is written with NO expirationTtl while every sibling in the same flow (smartcfg, access-token, pkce) IS written with one, asserted as an invariant over the write calls not a spot check; (2) the R2 mirror fires on initial connect and on rotation; (3) it does NOT fire on a refresh that returns no new refresh_token.

SLICE B — GRANTS_KV namespace separation (the structural fix): Mint a new KV namespace `GRANTS_KV` via the Cloudflare MCP `kv_namespace_create` (or `wrangler kv namespace create GRANTS_KV`) — this is a provisioning step doable by the MCP connector, not a human. Add the binding to wrangler.jsonc:15 alongside OAUTH_KV. Route ONLY the three grant-key operations (grantKey read at mychart.ts:166, writes at :236 and :1496) through a helper `grantStore(env) = env.GRANTS_KV ?? env.OAUTH_KV` so reads fall back to OAUTH_KV during migration and there is zero downtime. Ship a one-time idempotent migration that copies each existing `sux:mychart:grant:<org>` from OAUTH_KV to GRANTS_KV (read-if-absent-in-GRANTS_KV, then write), run once via a guarded admin path or a documented `wrangler kv key` copy for the 3 live orgs (uwmedicine/swedish/evergreen). Leave OAUTH_KV copies in place (harmless) or delete after verify. Add a test asserting grant reads/writes target GRANTS_KV when bound. This makes any namespace-level "clear the cache" on OAUTH_KV structurally unable to reach the irreplaceable grant.

Rationale for building both, not just one: Slice A makes loss survivable (recoverable from R2), Slice B makes it structurally unlikely (separate namespace). The grant is the mechanism the ~Aug-15 UW Med record recovery depends on; belt-and-suspenders is warranted for an irreplaceable, rarely-rotating, tiny secret.
evidence: Live HEAD origin/main @ 2984d84d. wrangler.jsonc:15 shows single kv_namespaces binding OAUTH_KV=d0dadc11fdd64598992f4a84a958bafb, no GRANTS_KV. grep of sux/src/mychart.ts: grant writes at :236 and :1496 write to OAUTH_KV with no expirationTtl and no R2/backup call; no backupGrant symbol exists in source (only test fixtures reference sux:mychart:grant). env.R2 already bound (mychart.ts:312, PHI writes). PR #1556 (the bot's claimed R2-mirror partial) is state=CLOSED, mergedAt=null — it never merged, so nothing from this issue is on HEAD. gh pr search for GRANTS_KV/1460 returns []. Issue cycled claim→attempt→drop 2x and got needs-human solely because the build sandbox can't run wrangler kv namespace create — a sandbox-credential gap, not an AI-impossible action (Cloudflare MCP kv_namespace_create is available in this org's connector set).

rationale: The only stated blocker is "suxlib is read-only from this sandbox (push 403)". That is a property of the autonomous build sandbox's token scope, NOT a genuine hard block: SuxOS/suxlib is a normal repo with a live git remote (git@github.com:SuxOS/suxlib.git) and a standard PR/CI pipeline. The needs-human label is therefore mis-applied — a competent engineer (or a builder dispatched to the correct repo) can land this. The bug is fully diagnosed and the fix is small and self-contained. DECISION (design/scope): replace the single `JSON.parse(s)` attempt in parseScalar's `s.startsWith('[')||s.startsWith('{')` branch with a proper recursive-descent YAML flow-collection parser. Algorithm: (1) keep the existing `JSON.parse` fast-path as a first try for genuinely-valid-JSON flow collections (preserves current passing behavior/tests incl. #345); (2) on JSON.parse failure, tokenize the flow collection respecting nesting depth for `[]`/`{}` and quotes (single/double), splitting top-level elements on commas; (3) recurse each element through parseScalar so unquoted scalars (`x`->string 'x', `1`->number, `true`->bool, `~`/`null`->null), quoted elements, nested flow collections, and empty collections `[]`/`{}` all resolve correctly; for maps, split each top-level element on the first top-level `:` (reuse the quote-aware splitMappingKey logic already in the file) and parseScalar both key and value; (4) if the collection is malformed/unbalanced, fall through to `return s` as today (never throw). Add tests in test/domain/transform.test.ts: parseYaml('b: [x, y]')==={b:['x','y']}, 'b: {a: 1, c: hi}'==={b:{a:1,c:'hi'}}, nested 'b: [[x], [y, z]]', empty 'b: []'/'b: {}', mixed quoted/unquoted 'b: [x, "y, z", 3]'. Scope is one file + one test file; no API/interface change.

JSON.parse('[x, y]') throws on the bare identifier x, so the raw string is returned — matches the issue exactly. The `#345` flow-depth logic (lines 144-169) lives only in stripYamlComment (comment stripping), not value parsing. `gh pr list --repo SuxOS/suxlib --state open` = [] and `--state all --search "flow yaml sequence parseScalar"` = [] → no sibling/merged fix. `grep -rn 1399` in suxlib src/test = no hits → not implemented. decision: Build a KV export/restore sub-job on the existing daily cron, decisions locked as follows: (1) ENCRYPTION — do NOT introduce a bespoke encryption key/secret for v1. R2 is already server-side AES-256 encrypted at rest and OAUTH_KV lives in the same Cloudflare account/trust boundary, so the offsite copy inherits at-rest encryption; the issue's "encrypted at rest" requirement is satisfied by R2 SSE. (This is the atom the bot wrongly called a hard block.) Application-level envelope encryption is a separable future follow-up, explicitly out of scope. (2) SCOPE — there is exactly ONE KV namespace to protect: OAUTH_KV (binding OAUTH_KV, id d0dadc11fdd64598992f4a84a958bafb). Export enumerates it whole. (3) EXPORT — new sub-job `kv_export` added to maintenanceTick() in sux/src/index.ts via runSubJob, in a new module sux/src/fns/_kv_backup.ts. Enumerate with env.OAUTH_KV.list({cursor, limit:1000}) looping on list_complete/page.cursor (exact pattern from tasks.ts listTasks), get() each key's value+metadata, stream as newline-delimited JSON ({key,value,metadata,expiration}) into R2 binding R2 (bucket sux-mcp) at kv-export/<YYYY-MM-DD>/dump.ndjson plus a manifest.json (key count, bytes, completed-at). Bound work per tick and persist the cursor under a KV key (e.g. sux:kvbackup:cursor) so an interrupted run resumes next tick rather than restarting; write a sux:kvbackup:last-complete timestamp when a full pass finishes. (4) RESTORE — a `kv_restore` op/fn reading kv-export/<date>/dump.ndjson from R2 and PUT-ing keys back; default dry-run:true (reports counts/diff, writes nothing), requires explicit dry_run:false + a date arg to actually write; never deletes keys not present in the dump. (5) STALENESS — emit gauge sux_kv_export_age_hours (age of newest kv-export/ object) on the existing shipMetricsSnapshot push (dormant unless GRAFANA_PROM_* set, matching gh_actions_billing), and console.warn when age>48h so it also surfaces in logs/Analytics; the Grafana alert RULE on >48h is a one-line dashboard config, not code, left as a follow-up note. Restore counterpart and dry-run gate make the whole thing reversible.
rationale: Every requirement maps onto a pattern already live in the repo, and the sole cited hard-block dissolves under scrutiny. The bot dropped this only because it kept landing in a 12-issue batch that timed out at 30 min (see the cancelled-timeout comment) — a batching/sizing failure, not an unbuildable feature. With Colin's "you choose all human-required" grant, I make the load-bearing call — rely on R2 SSE, no new encryption secret — which removes the one atom the bot claimed the sandbox couldn't provision. What remains is ordinary Workers code: cursor-paginated KV enumeration (verbatim tasks.ts pattern), an R2 write under kv-export/<date>/, a dry-run-default restore, and a metrics-gauge staleness signal on the existing snapshot push. A single dedicated build session clears it easily.

decision: Make oracle URL ingest truncation-honest and raise the fetch ceiling, WITHOUT the full spec rewrite. Concrete slice (sux/src/fns/oracle.ts + sux/src/fns/_util.ts + oracle.test.ts): (1) Add `truncated?: boolean` to the `Fetched` type (_util.ts:103); have `readBodyText` (_util.ts:151) return whether it hit the cap — it already detects `consumed >= maxBytes`, just surface it — and thread that flag through `fetchText`. (2) Raise oracle's `FETCH_CAP` (oracle.ts:67) from 40_000 to 400_000 chars: safe because downstream is already segmented (splitPayload/PIECE_MAX) and the model never sees more than DISTILL_INPUT_CAP=24_000 per piece; 400k covers a typical Gutenberg book and stays well under _util's 2_000_000 default and bounds isolate memory. (3) In the learn receipt (oracle.ts:303 return and the `ok(oj({...}))` at ~507) add `truncated` and `fetched_bytes`/`cap`; when truncated, the receipt says so loudly (e.g. `truncated:true, note:"source exceeded 400000 bytes; ingested first 400000 — re-learn segmented or raise cap"`) — satisfying requested item #3 (fail loudly, never silently ingest the wrong content) with zero quarantine/verify/quality-score machinery. (4) Test: a >cap URL body → receipt `truncated:true`; a <cap body → truncated absent/false. Skip the optional Gutenberg-front-matter stripping (item #4) — YAGNI for this slice. This directly kills the silent-corruption class the issue title names.

decision:
Build a new host service `services/ctrld-sync/` modeled byte-for-byte on ecs-autoupdate's proven API-PUT pattern (POSIX/busybox-ash, `set -u` not `set -e`, token read 0600 from /etc/controld/api.token, curl to https://api.controld.com/profiles/{PK}/options/..., local change-cache to avoid needless writes). Scope to ONE bounded, gate-greenable slice:

SLICE 1 (dispatch this):
- Repo carries the DESIRED config as a checked-in file `services/ctrld-sync/etc/ctrld-sync.d/options.json` — a POSITIVE ALLOWLIST of a small set of scalar profile *option* keys and their desired {status,value,custom_value}. Upstreams and rules are EXPLICITLY out of scope for slice 1.
- `usr/sbin/ctrld-sync`: for each allowlisted option, GET the live profile option, diff desired-vs-live, and PUT only the delta. Skip unchanged options via the local cache.
- DRY-RUN IS THE DEFAULT: bare invocation (and `--dry-run`) prints the intended diff, exits 0, touches neither the API nor the cache. A write requires an explicit `--apply` flag.
- SACRED guards, enforced in code + a shellcheck/ucode-testable pure decision fn: (a) the allowlist is closed — any key not in options.json is NEVER written, so upstreams can't be touched; (b) hard-refuse any PUT whose target path matches `upstreams` or whose custom_value/value would be empty/whitespace (never null a field); (c) never touch the local ctrld-static.toml autogen sections or the `[service]` overlay — this tool speaks ONLY to the cloud profile API.
- Service wiring mirroring ecs-autoupdate: `etc/init.d/ctrld-sync`, `etc/uci-defaults/99-ctrld-sync`, `etc/ctrld-sync.conf.example` (CONTROLD_PROFILE_PK, token path, API base overridable), and a README documenting the dry-run-first contract and the allowlist model. Reuse ecs-autoupdate's conf/lock/state conventions.

DEFER to follow-up slices (file as children of #670, do NOT build now): a cron/hotplug trigger cadence (slice 2); managing upstreams/rules with an even stricter never-empty guard (slice 3); bidirectional reconcile / drift reporting (slice 4). Keeping slice 1 to one script + one declared file + README + wiring is what makes it fit the 30-min build budget the monolith blew.

Build ucode with an overlay `overrideAttrs` on nixpkgs' `ucode` derivation, swapping `src = fetchFromGitHub { owner="jow-"; repo="ucode"; rev="85922056ef7abeace3cca3ab28bc1ac2d88e31b1"; hash=<lib.fakeHash → real hash from first build>; }` (nixpkgs' derivation already handles json-c, libucode.so, and the fs module correctly, so the CI's manual .so/fs.so gymnastics are unnecessary here). Commit `flake.lock`. Add a one-paragraph README/nix note documenting `nix develop` + that the rev must stay in lockstep with `ci.yml`'s pin and the box's OpenWrt release. Do NOT touch ci.yml (the #665 pin stays as-is). EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE for this build (defer to separate follow-up issues): (a) `packages.` pkgsStatic static-musl /opt tool builds — deferred until a specific tool actually needs to ship to /opt (YAGNI today, no named consumer); (b) nvfetcher/equivalent auto-tracking of OpenWrt's ucode Makefile pin — low value since the pin moves only deliberately per OpenWrt release; (c) the advisory standalone CI job entering the devShell to run `ucode -c` — deferred because ci.yml's #665 from-source build already runs exactly that check, so it is pure duplication until the flake is proven and the from-source step is ready to collapse onto it. This slice fits the 30-min builder window (a single flake.nix + lock + one hash + a doc note) which the full plan repeatedly did not.
rationale: The issue is a legitimate durable-infra task blocked only by oversized scope (5 consecutive 30-min timeouts, then needs-human), not by any human-only act — so under full delegated authority the correct move is to make the scoping call rather than punt to a human or a scoping doc. The acute problem (CI↔box ucode drift, the outage cause) is ALREADY fixed on HEAD by #665, which removes urgency and lets me trim the plan to its one genuinely additive, builder-sized piece: a reproducible local devShell running the box-exact ucode (today devs have NO local ucode check; CI is the only gate). The other three parts are deferrable: pkgsStatic /opt builds are YAGNI with no named tool, nvfetcher auto-tracks a deliberately-static pin, and the advisory CI job duplicates the working #665 check. Rewriting the body to this minimal scope is what makes it dispatch-ready — the same builder that timed out on the full plan can complete a single flake.nix + lock + hash + doc note.

recommended_action: Relabel (remove needs-human) and SPLIT into 3 dispatchable sub-issues; keep the ingest run and live-serve as out-of-band human atoms. Do NOT re-dispatch #41 as-is (oversized, times out, and its data-load leg is unbuildable in the sandbox).
decision: Architecture: the Worker never reads the private suxvault repo at request time. Introduce a `VaultSource` abstraction backed by two new bindings — Vectorize index `suxvault-notes` (retrieval) + an R2 content store (`SUXVAULT_CONTENT`, record bodies/citation targets). (1) Replace STUB_ENTRIES: `getNavigatorView` lists/queries VaultSource → maps real records (records/knowledge/people/values/incidents) to NavigatorEntry with real citationIds resolving to real suxvault paths. (2) Re-derive #30/#31 (PR #34 is closed): `askQuestion` = citation-constrained retrieval over the same VaultSource, never fabricating an answer/citation. (3) Gate all of it behind a `USE_REAL_VAULT` flag defaulting OFF so the public *.workers.dev staging Worker keeps serving synthetic demoData.ts (honors the wrangler.jsonc PHI rail). (4) Test entirely against a small vendored fixture manifest (mirror ~5 real record shapes from suxvault's docs/vault-migration-manifest.md schema) — no live suxvault needed to build/test. The actual suxvault→Vectorize/R2 ingest is a SEPARATE out-of-band script (runs where it has suxvault access), and real live serving stays behind #35 login + Cloudflare Access + suxos.net (never public workers.dev, PHI at-rest class stated).
rationale: STUB_ENTRIES + qa stub are confirmed live on HEAD, so the issue is genuinely open — but as-worded it is unbuildable by the pipeline for two reasons a competent engineer must resolve first: the builder token 404s on SuxOS/suxvault (repo's own CLAUDE.md, #46), and the #30/#31 retrieval foundation it reuses is now closed-unmerged (PR #34, CONFLICTING). The fix is a decision, not a human act: decouple the Worker-side wiring (fully buildable behind a flag, against fixtures) from the data-load (needs suxvault access) and the live PHI serve (needs Access/DNS + Colin's go). Split into (a) VaultSource abstraction + Vectorize/R2 bindings + fixture manifest; (b) real navigator wiring behind USE_REAL_VAULT (default off); (c) re-derived citation-constrained /api/qa retrieval. All three are small, independently gate-passing, and dispatchable — unlike the current single oversized issue that times out. Not dispatch_ready as one unit; ready once split.
evidence: gh + git on live HEAD: `grep STUB_ENTRIES` → still in src/navigator.ts:42; src/qa.ts still status:not_implemented. `gh pr list --state open` → []. PR #34 = CLOSED, mergeable CONFLICTING, never merged (Vectorize/embeddings foundation). `git ls-tree -r origin/main` shows src/auth,review,audit,references present but NO src/embeddings, no Vectorize/R2/suxvault binding in wrangler.jsonc (only NAV_CACHE + RATE_LIMITER). Repo CLAUDE.md documents suxvault token 404 (#46) and the #30/#31/#32/#37/#38 blocked cluster. Colin's reopen comment confirms false-completion (PR #45 only touched toneHighlighter/demo) and names the real blocker as unresolved suxvault repo-access. Issue labels: needs-human + v1.2; 10+ failed auto-builds then built-alone 30-min timeout.
hard_block_action: Two residual atoms remain outside the pipeline after the buildable slices land: (1) grant the suxbot GitHub-App installation read access to SuxOS/suxvault (org-admin change) OR run the ingest from a session that already has suxvault access (e.g. the sux vault connector), to populate Vectorize `suxvault-notes` + R2 SUXVAULT_CONTENT; (2) Colin's authorization to serve real patient PHI on a live endpoint — provision Cloudflare Access recipients + suxos.net DNS cutover + stated at-rest class (never public *.workers.dev). Neither blocks the Worker-side code.
local_continuation: Build slices (a)/(b)/(c) above: VaultSource interface + Vectorize/R2 bindings in wrangler.jsonc, getNavigatorView + askQuestion reading from VaultSource behind USE_REAL_VAULT (default off), citation resolution to real suxvault paths, full test coverage against a vendored fixture manifest, plus a standalone `scripts/ingest-suxvault.ts` that reads suxvault and writes Vectorize+R2 (runnable once access exists). Result: everything but the access grant and the live-PHI go is done and merged; flipping USE_REAL_VAULT on a properly-fronted prod Worker after ingest is the only remaining step.

recommended_action: SPLIT the rollup: (1) make the decidable calls now and refile them as buildable pipeline issues; (2) keep #641 as the residual on-box tracker for the genuinely physical-router items. Concretely — DECIDE the dead .p2p upstream = DELETE (it was kadnode, decommissioned; every repo design doc agrees "no listener on :3535 → remove `server '/p2p/::1#3535'`"); UN-GATE #520 + #525 (their only cited blocker, "elog() not on main," is false on HEAD — elog() is at watchdog/usr/lib/suxwatch/lib.sh:45; they are repo-only suxwatch code changes, dispatch them); DEFER #557 to a normal buildable telemetry issue. Leave the true box-session atoms (#486/#471/#416/#446 and #631's authenticated-m@-through-Access verify) on #641.
rationale: #641 is not stale-because-children-closed: the 8 folded issues were closed NOT_PLANNED at the same minute #641 was authored (06:47-06:48Z, KISS-triage consolidation), so #641 is the live tracker and the work is still open. Of its ~9 line items, three are pure decisions or already-answered-on-HEAD and do NOT need the physical box: (a) the .p2p forward — the repo's own uci-correctness-audit.md/dns-rework-deploy-plan.md/cycle2-deploy-plan.md all independently document that :3535 was kadnode, kadnode is gone, nothing binds the port, and the fix is to remove the line, so Colin's two-way decision resolves deterministically to DELETE; (b/c) #520 and #525 are gated only on the false claim that elog() isn't on main — it is, so un-gate and let the pipeline build the remaining elog() call-sites. The rest are genuinely box-bound (SSH to the home OpenWrt router to capture live /etc state + live dig, or a real-Chrome authenticated pass through Cloudflare Access as m@) — those bounced 8-9 auto-builds because the cloud plane has no route to the physical device, and stay on #641 as the real needs-human residual. A rollup can't dispatch to one builder as-is, hence not dispatch_ready; splitting off the three decidable items drains most of the queue now.
